articles.title: U.S. Expands AI Model Testing to Boost Cybersecurity
articles.description: The U.S. government has extended its program to assess unreleased AI models, including those from Google’s DeepMind, xAI, and Microsoft, to mitigate risks such as cyberattacks. OpenAI and Anthropic were already cooperating voluntarily. The initiative aims to curb misuse of AI in cyber warfare and biosecurity threats.
articles.content:
The Trump administration announced on Tuesday its expanded initiative to enable U.S. government scientists to evaluate unreleased artificial intelligence models. This program, now includes major players such as Google’s DeepMind, xAI, and Microsoft.

articles.title: It's transparency, not tech alone, that drives crypto adoption, panelists tell Consensus Miami
articles.description: Execs from PayPal, Robinhood, Public.com and 248 Ventures said the way to move retail into crypto and AI is to slow down, show your work, and put users back in control.
articles.content:
The path to mainstream crypto adoption runs through more visible, controllable product design, executives from PayPal, Robinhood, Public.com and 248 Ventures told CoinDesk’s Consensus Miami conference Tuesday.

articles.title: Crypto exchange Coinbase to lay off 14% of staff as AI reshapes work
articles.description: The company, formerly headquartered in San Francisco, said that it's also changing the way it operates and will lean on smaller teams that can do more with AI.
articles.content:
Cryptocurrency exchange Coinbase said it’s slashing roughly 14% of its workforce or about 700 workers, partly because artificial intelligence is reshaping the way people work.

articles.description: Hong Kong has seen a string of blockbuster listings by Chinese AI firms in recent months. Yet the Hang Seng Tech Index remains deep in a slump.
articles.content:

articles.title: Year Partnership with Holyoke Medical Center to Reduce Administrative Burden
articles.description: IKS Health, the healthcare arm of Inventurus Knowledge Solutions Limited, announced a strategic multi-year clinical and revenue optimization platform partnership with Holyoke Medical Center, a 219-bed independent, non-profit community hospital in Western Massachusetts, on May 5, 2026. The partnership aims to reduce clinical and administrative burden, lower operating costs, and unlock new revenue opportunities for the hospital, which employs over 1,800 staff and more than 375 physicians. The collaboration leverages IKS Health's Care Enablement platform, combining agentic AI workflows with human expertise to streamline clinical and financial operations. IKS Health, founded in 2006, is recognized by Black Book Research as the top provider of AI-driven RCM services and by Google Cloud with a DORA Award for augmenting human expertise with AI.
